content.text: Sequencer
The sequencer is the heart of Circuit Tracks. Each of the six tracks has its own 32-step sequencer with advanced features like probability, micro-timing, and parameter locks.
Transport Controls
Use the Play and Record buttons to control playback and recording.

items: Play: Start/stop playback. The sequencer loops continuously.
Record: Enable recording. Notes you play will be quantized to the grid.
Shift + Play: Stop and reset to step 1.

content.text: Step Editing
The 32 pads represent the 32 steps in the sequence. Lit pads have notes programmed; dim pads are empty.
By default you see steps 1-16. Press and hold a step on the top row and tap a step on the bottom row to quickly jump to steps 17-32.
To add a note: Hold an empty step and play a note on another pad (in note view) or turn the macro knobs to set pitch.
Recording
With Record enabled, any notes you play will be recorded to the sequencer in real-time. Notes are quantized to the nearest step.

content.title: Pro Tip
content.text: You can record while the sequencer is stopped. Press Record, play your part, then press Play to hear it back.
Velocity
Press the Velocity button to view and edit velocity values. Each pad shows the velocity as a color intensity - brighter means louder.
To edit: Hold a step and turn Macro 1 to adjust its velocity.
Gate Length
Press the Length button to view and edit gate lengths. This controls how long each note plays.

items: Tied notes: Hold a step and tap subsequent steps to create long notes

content.text: Tempo
Tap the Tempo button repeatedly to set BPM by tapping. Or hold Tempo and turn Macro 1 to dial in a precise tempo.
Tempo range: 40-240 BPM. The display shows current BPM when Tempo is held.
Clearing Steps
Hold Clear and tap a step to remove the note. Hold Clear and press a track button to clear the entire track. Shift + Clear clears the whole pattern.

content.title: Be Careful
content.text: Shift + Clear cannot be undone for some operations. Use with caution!
Duplicate
The Duplicate button copies patterns or steps. Hold Duplicate and select a source, then select a destination.
